- Regenerate vectors in CI and diff against repo.
- Fail with a clear message on drift.

 {"id":"bd-23d7","title":"[IDEA-21.1] Implement zone policy diff + rollback CLI","description":"## What
Implement `fcp policy diff` and `fcp policy rollback --plan`.

## How
- Load two policy objects by ObjectId.
- Produce a structured diff (added/removed/changed fields).
- Emit rollback plan object (not executed automatically).

## Success Criteria
- CLI outputs both human and JSON diffs.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- CLI returns JSON diff with `added/removed/changed` sections.
- Rollback plan includes previous policy ObjectId.

-{"id":"bd-24bc","title":"Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback","description":"# Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback

## Goal
Provide a safe, versioned workflow to propose, review, preview, and roll back policy changes across zones.

## Why (User Impact)
- Reduces accidental policy regressions.
- Enables deterministic review with clear diffs + risk summaries.
- Makes policy changes auditable and reversible.

## Scope
- PolicyBundle object (signed, versioned, immutable).
- Deterministic diff and preview engine.
- CLI workflow for create/preview/apply/rollback.

## Non-goals
- UI/editor; CLI only for now.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy bundles are signed and verifiable.
- Preview explains allow/deny deltas before apply.
- Rollback to previous bundle is one command.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:28.006280446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:28.006280446Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-254d","title":"[IDEA-14] Connector Limits Constants Generator","description":"# Connector Limits Constants Generator

## Goal
Standardize platform-specific limits (message length, embed caps, payload sizes) via a generated `limits.rs` module per connector to avoid magic numbers.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram both require strict length limits; scattered constants are easy to miss. A canonical `limits.rs` improves correctness and testability.

## Scope
- Generator support in `fcp new` and documentation.
- Optional lint to ensure connectors define limits constants.

## Deliverables
- `limits.rs` template with TODO placeholders and defaults.
- Guidance on how to update limits when APIs change.

## Tests
- Unit test in generator to ensure limits file generated.
- Connector test helper verifying limit constants used in validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New connectors have a `limits.rs` by default.
- Validation functions use limits constants, not magic numbers.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:08.279393213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514131459Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-254d","depends_on_id":"bd-35ct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514069805Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32k7","title":"TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map","description":"# TEST-AUDIT: Coverage inventory + mock usage map

## Goal
Produce a concrete coverage and mock-usage inventory so we can target real-component tests first.

## Deliverables
- Coverage map by crate and module: fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-crypto, fcp-store, fcp-mesh, fcp-sandbox, fcp-host, fcp-cli, fcp-sdk, connectors.
- Mock/fake usage map: enumerate tests that rely on mock_server, wiremock, fake clients, or test doubles.
- Gap list for untested critical paths (protocol parse, crypto verification, revocation freshness, audit/receipt logic, sandbox enforcement).

## Notes
- This task does NOT delete existing mock tests; it produces a replacement plan for no-mock suites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One report file under docs/testing/ with per-crate coverage estimate and mock usage list.
- Each gap maps to a follow-up bead in this epic.

 {"id":"bd-32sh","title":"[IDEA-28.1] Implement usage metric schema + host aggregation","description":"## What
Implement usage metric schema and host aggregation pipeline.

## How
- Define `UsageMetric` and optional `CostEstimate` objects.
- SDK helper to emit metrics from connectors.
- Host aggregates per zone and stores rolling windows.

## Success Criteria
- Aggregated metrics accessible via API/CLI.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Aggregation windows are deterministic and bounded.
- Metrics emit confidence labels for cost estimates.

-{"id":"bd-334n","title":"Connector Lifecycle Manager: pin/canary/rollback","description":"# Connector Lifecycle Manager: pin/canary/rollback

## Goal
Enable safe connector upgrades with version pinning, staged rollout, and automatic rollback on health regressions.

## Why
- Prevents fleet-wide outages from bad releases.
- Supports offline environments via pinned artifacts.
- Gives operators deterministic control over connector versions.

## Scope
- Release metadata + rollout policies.
- Host rollout controller + health gating.
- CLI workflow + reporting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canary rollout can be configured per connector.
- Automatic rollback triggers on health thresholds.
- Operators can pin and unpin versions deterministically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:07.205546340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:07.205546340Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-33no","title":"Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate","description":"# Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate

## Goal
Block untrusted connector installs/updates by verifying signed supply-chain attestations and SBOMs before activation.

## Why (User Impact)
- Operators need confidence that connector binaries match audited provenance.
- Prevents downgrade/typosquat attacks and malicious binaries.
- Makes offline installs safer via cached, signed artifacts.

## Scope
- Attestation + SBOM object schemas, signature verification, and policy gating in fcp-host.
- CLI support for verify/install workflows and report generation.
- Structured evidence bundle for audits.

## Non-goals
- Writing a full vulnerability scanner (use external tools; we only ingest/report results).
- Replacing the registry; this is a gate on install/update.

## Deliverables
- Attestation/SBOM object schema + verification rules.
- Host-side gating on install/update.
- CLI verify/report commands.
- Unit/integration + E2E scripts with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A connector with valid attestation+SBOM installs; invalid/unsigned is rejected.
- Verification is deterministic and produces an evidence bundle.
- Errors are actionable and redact secrets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:43.109587772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:43.109587772Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-34lm","title":"CLI: fcp connector (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery","description":"# CLI: `fcp connector` (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery

## Goal
Implement connector discovery commands that agents (and operators) use to find and understand available connectors before invoking tools.

This fills the gap between Agent Integration (`1n78.30`) which defines the discovery API and the CLI which needs to expose it.

## Commands

### `fcp connector list`
List all registered connectors with summary info.

```bash
# Human output
fcp connector list
# ID              NAME          VERSION   TOOLS   RISK    STATUS
# fcp.discord     Discord       1.2.0     12      Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.github      GitHub        2.0.1     24      Risky   ⚠ Degraded (rate limited)
# fcp.slack       Slack         1.0.0     8       Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.anthropic   Anthropic     1.0.0     3       Safe    ✓ Healthy

# Filter by category
fcp connector list --category messaging
fcp connector list --category llm

# Filter by max risk tier
fcp connector list --max-risk safe
fcp connector list --max-risk moderate

# Filter by health
fcp connector list --healthy
fcp connector list --degraded

# JSON output for agents
fcp connector list --json
```

### `fcp connector info <connector>`
Show detailed connector information including manifest metadata.

```bash
fcp connector info fcp.discord
# Connector: fcp.discord
# Name: Discord
# Version: 1.2.0
# Archetype: Bidirectional
# Home Zone: z:private
# 
# Capabilities Required:
#   - discord.messages.read (Safe)
#   - discord.messages.send (Risky)
#   - discord.webhooks.manage (Dangerous)
# 
# Network Constraints:
#   Allowed: discord.com:443, gateway.discord.gg:443
#   CIDR Deny: default (RFC1918 + localhost)
# 
# State Model: singleton_writer
# Event Topics: message.new, message.edited, member.joined
# 
# Health: Healthy
# Uptime: 3d 14h 22m
# Last Health Check: 2s ago

fcp connector info fcp.discord --json
```

### `fcp connector introspect <connector>`
Return MCP-compatible tool descriptors for a connector.

```bash
# Human-readable tool list
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord
# Tools (12):
#   send_message      Send a message to a channel           Risky
#   send_dm           Send a direct message                 Risky  
#   list_channels     List channels in a guild              Safe
#   get_message       Get a specific message                Safe
#   ...

# Full MCP tool descriptors (JSON)
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --json

# Single tool
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --tool send_message --json
```

## Output Contracts

### ConnectorSummary (list)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorSummary {
    pub id: ConnectorId,
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub version: semver::Version,
    pub categories: Vec<String>,
    pub tool_count: u32,
    pub max_risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub health: ConnectorHealth,
}
```

### ConnectorInfo (info)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorInfo {
    pub summary: ConnectorSummary,
    pub archetype: ConnectorArchetype,
    pub home_zone: ZoneId,
    pub capabilities: CapabilityDeclarations,
    pub network_constraints: NetworkConstraints,
    pub state_model: ConnectorStateModel,
    pub event_topics: Vec<EventTopic>,
    pub manifest_hash: ObjectId,
    pub installed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

### ToolDescriptor (introspect) - SEP-1382 compliant
```rust
pub struct ToolDescriptor {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub output_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub requires_confirmation: bool,
    pub idempotent: bool,
    pub supports_simulate: bool,
    pub latency_hint: LatencyHint,
    pub rate_limits: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub examples: Option<Vec<ToolExample>>,
    pub ai_hints: Option<String>,
}
```

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- test_connector_list_filters_by_category
- test_connector_list_filters_by_risk
- test_connector_list_filters_by_health
- test_connector_info_includes_all_manifest_data
- test_connector_introspect_returns_mcp_compliant_descriptors
- test_connector_introspect_single_tool

### Integration Tests
- test_connector_list_matches_registry
- test_connector_introspect_schema_valid

### Snapshot Tests
- connector_list_human.snap
- connector_list_json.snap
- connector_info_human.snap
- connector_info_json.snap
- connector_introspect_human.snap
- connector_introspect_json.snap

### E2E Script
e2e/connector_discovery.sh with detailed logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fcp connector list` returns all registered connectors
- [ ] Filtering by category, risk, and health works correctly
- [ ] `fcp connector info` returns full manifest-derived data
- [ ] `fcp connector introspect` returns MCP-compliant tool descriptors
- [ ] All commands support `--json` output
- [ ] No secrets are included in any output

-{"id":"bd-3iac","title":"Capability Usage Analytics + Least-Privilege Advisor","description":"# Capability Usage Analytics + Least-Privilege Advisor

## Goal
Measure real capability usage and suggest minimal capability sets per zone/connector.

## Why
- Reduces over-provisioned capabilities.
- Enables least-privilege hardening over time.
- Provides actionable operator insights.

## Scope
- Usage telemetry collection.
- Analyzer to suggest minimal capability set.
- CLI reports.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reports list unused capabilities and suggested reductions.
- Suggestions are deterministic and explainable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:05.238933010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:07:05.238933010Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3m2a","title":"TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)","description":"# TEST-CONNECTORS-LIVE: Real-service integration suites (manual)

## Goal
Add live integration tests for external services without mocks/fakes, using dedicated sandbox accounts.

## Scope
- Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram, Discord, Twitter connectors.
- Real API calls with rate limits and secret handling.

## Constraints
- Must run as manual-only or gated by secrets in CI.
- No production accounts, no recording or fake fixtures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each connector has a live test plan and minimal smoke tests.
- Logs redact secrets and include correlation IDs and cost/latency metadata.

 {"id":"bd-3myr","title":"[SC] Attestation + SBOM object schemas","description":"## Goal
Define canonical schemas for SupplyChainAttestation and SBOM objects plus validation rules.

## Deliverables
- New object schemas + schema IDs (CBOR/JSON) for:
  - SupplyChainAttestation (SLSA/in-toto style provenance)
  - SBOM (CycloneDX or SPDX subset; immutable)
- Signature requirements + key binding rules.
- Canonical hash rules for determinism.

## Tests
- Golden vectors for valid/invalid attestations.
- Schema hash determinism tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schemas are versioned and validated by conformance tooling.
- Deterministic encoding + hash is stable across runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:57.665118567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:57.678169880Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3myr","depends_on_id":"bd-33no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:57.678114036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rgx","title":"[SC] Host verification pipeline + policy gate","description":"## Goal
Verify attestation + SBOM before connector install/upgrade; enforce policy gate in fcp-host.

## Deliverables
- Verification engine: signature checks, provenance matching, SBOM integrity.
- Policy config: require_attestation, min_slsa_level, allow_unsigned for dev.
- Evidence bundle generation (JSON + hashes) stored as mesh objects.

## Tests
- Unit tests for verification rules.
- Integration tests with test connectors (valid/invalid).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid or missing attestations block install unless policy allows.
- Evidence bundle is deterministic and audit-ready.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:10.158698768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:57.094045783Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rgx","depends_on_id":"bd-33no","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:10.172243177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rgx","depends_on_id":"bd-3myr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:54.115632652Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rgx","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:57.094006460Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3tja","title":"Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger","description":"# Mesh Trace Capture + Replay Debugger

## Goal
Enable deterministic capture and replay of mesh traffic for debugging and incident analysis.

## Why
- Reproduce complex mesh failures offline.
- Provide evidence bundles for audits and regressions.
- Improve developer velocity for hard-to-debug issues.

## Scope
- Trace format with redaction.
- Capture hooks in MeshNode.
- Replay CLI and deterministic playback.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Captures can be replayed to reproduce routing/admission decisions.
- Sensitive fields are redacted or encrypted.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:06.943101048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:06.943101048Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3zhv","title":"[IDEA-01.4] SDK runtime kit: tests + reference examples","description":"## Goal
Provide reference examples and comprehensive tests for the runtime kit.

## Details
- Add a minimal streaming example (fake gateway) and polling example (fake API) in testkit.
- Add golden-log snapshots for health transitions (dependent on bd-11g2 schema).
- Document usage patterns in `docs/STUDY_Clawdbot_Connector_Patterns.md` and/or SDK README.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests for both supervisors.
- Validate logging against JSONL schema (once available).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Examples compile and can be used as copy/paste templates by connector authors.
- Tests cover common failure modes (timeouts, 429, disconnect, resume).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:27:46.561879044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:32:13.520423475Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:32:13.520352523Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3zhv","depends_on_id":"bd-11g2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:28:34.508761764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3zhv","depends_on_id":"bd-2zy9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:27:46.571210031Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-3zhv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation Complete - Created supervisor_examples.rs in fcp-testkit with:

1. FakePollingApi - configurable mock API with error injection (timeout, rate limit, auth failed, network error)
2. FakePollingConnector - reference connector using PollingSupervisor
3. FakeStreamingSession - reference session implementing StreamingSession trait
4. FakeUpdate / FakeStreamEvent - example event types

Added 8 tests covering API success, error injection, rate limiting, fatal errors, and connector shutdown/failure modes.

Also fixed pre-existing test failures in policy_simulation.rs (missing requires_posture and posture_attestation fields).

All 36 fcp-testkit tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:31:51Z"}]}
